Title: The PavDREB1E–PavD8–PavHY5 module integrates light and gibberellin signals to regulate sweet cherry anthocyanin biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher expression than PavRGA in fruits; protein stabilized under low GA3/light-favored conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a positive coactivator of anthocyanin biosynthesis by enhancing PavDREB1E binding and transcriptional activation of PavCHS, PavDFR, and PavUFGT.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LCI; Y2H; pull-down; EMSA enhancement assay; dual-LUC; cell-free degradation assay; in vitro ubiquitination
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavD8 is the major fruit-expressed DELLA chosen from the sweet cherry DELLA family. 2. It directly interacts with PavDREB1E. 3. PavD8 enhances PavDREB1E binding to the promoters of PavCHS, PavDFR, and PavUFGT and boosts transcriptional activation. 4. PavHY5 strengthens the PavD8–PavDREB1E interaction. 5. PavCOP1-2 ubiquitinates PavD8, and PavD8 is less stable in darkness/high-GA contexts.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light exposure; darkness; low GA3; GA3 treatment; MG132 in degradation assay
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although a DELLA signaling protein rather than a transcription factor, PavD8 is a central core component linking GA status to anthocyanin biosynthesis. It was stabilized under low GA3 and destabilized when GA3 is elevated.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Light-induced expression of PavCHS is associated with increased anthocyanin accumulation; it is directly activated by PavDREB1E.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit peel under light exposure; downregulated by GA3 and upregulated by PAC
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC reporter assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavCHS is a key anthocyanin biosynthetic gene induced by light in Rainier fruit. 2. PavDREB1E directly binds the PavCHS promoter and activates its transcription. 3. PavD8 strengthens PavDREB1E-mediated activation of the PavCHS promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light exposure; darkness; GA3 treatment; paclobutrazol treatment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated as a direct downstream target gene of the regulatory module rather than an experimentally manipulated focal core gene.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Light-induced expression of PavDFR is associated with anthocyanin accumulation; it is directly activated by PavDREB1E.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit peel under light exposure; downregulated by GA3 and upregulated by PAC
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC reporter assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavDFR is induced by light during fruit coloration. 2. PavDREB1E directly binds the PavDFR promoter and activates transcription. 3. PavD8 enhances PavDREB1E activation of the PavDFR promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light exposure; darkness; GA3 treatment; paclobutrazol treatment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct downstream target, not a primary experimentally manipulated core gene.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PavUFGT is strongly induced by light and directly activated by PavDREB1E, promoting anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit peel under light exposure; transcript increased >2000-fold after light exposure; downregulated by GA3 and upregulated by PAC
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC reporter assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavUFGT is the most strongly light-induced anthocyanin biosynthesis gene reported in the study. 2. PavDREB1E directly binds the PavUFGT promoter and activates its transcription. 3. PavD8 further strengthens PavDREB1E-mediated activation of PavUFGT.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light exposure; darkness; GA3 treatment; paclobutrazol treatment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct downstream target, not a primary experimentally manipulated core gene.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: strongly induced by light in fruit; upregulated by PAC; downregulated by GA3; low in darkness
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: light; low GA3; paclobutrazol; darkness represses transcript accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates PavCHS, PavDFR, and PavUFGT by directly binding their promoters
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms a functional module with PavD8 and PavHY5; interaction with PavD8 is enhanced by PavHY5
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: light-responsive positive regulator that directly activates key anthocyanin structural genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes light-induced anthocyanin accumulation and fruit coloration.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression; RNAi silencing; RT-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC; Y2H; pull-down; LCI; cell-free degradation assay; in vitro ubiquitination
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavDREB1E is the most light-induced DREB gene in sweet cherry fruit. 2. It positively regulates anthocyanin accumulation, as silencing reduces and overexpression enhances fruit coloration. 3. It directly binds and activates the promoters of PavCHS, PavDFR, and PavUFGT. 4. PavD8 enhances its DNA-binding and transcriptional activation activity, and PavHY5 strengthens the PavD8–PavDREB1E interaction. 5. PavCOP1-1 targets PavDREB1E for ubiquitination and degradation, especially in darkness.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light exposure; darkness; GA3 treatment; paclobutrazol treatment; MG132 in degradation assay
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used for overexpression and reporter/effector assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PavDREB1E did not bind the ABA biosynthesis gene PavNCED1 promoter in Y1H assays.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced by light; low transcript levels in darkness
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: indirectly promotes expression of PavCHS, PavDFR, and PavUFGT by strengthening PavD8–PavDREB1E interaction
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: part of the PavDREB1E–PavD8–PavHY5 module; interacts with PavD8 and PavDREB1E
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: light-induced positive cofactor that enhances assembly/activity of the anthocyanin-activating module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression enhances fruit coloration and anthocyanin accumulation under light.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression; RT-qPCR; pull-down; LCI; cell-free degradation assay; in vitro ubiquitination
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavHY5 is induced by light and localizes to the nucleus. 2. Overexpression promotes anthocyanin accumulation and fruit coloration. 3. PavHY5 interacts with both PavD8 and PavDREB1E. 4. It strengthens the PavD8–PavDREB1E interaction, thereby enhancing anthocyanin biosynthesis. 5. Both PavCOP1-1 and PavCOP1-2 ubiquitinate PavHY5.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light exposure; darkness; MG132 in degradation assay
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No accession number provided in the supplied text.

Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: darkness/light signaling; likely dark-induced nuclear accumulation as discussed
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: indirectly represses anthocyanin biosynthesis by destabilizing positive regulators
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: anthocyanin biosynthesis via proteostasis of transcriptional activators
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: negative regulator that suppresses fruit coloration by ubiquitinating positive regulators
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inhibits anthocyanin accumulation by ubiquitinating and promoting degradation of PavDREB1E and PavHY5.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LCI; Y2H; pull-down; in vitro ubiquitination; cell-free degradation context
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavCOP1-1 directly interacts with PavDREB1E and PavHY5. 2. It ubiquitinates PavDREB1E and PavHY5 in vitro. 3. Its action contributes to degradation of positive anthocyanin regulators, especially in darkness.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light versus dark conditions; MG132 in degradation assay
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper infers light-induced nuclear depletion/cytosolic relocation consistent with COP1 function, but direct subcellular dynamics for PavCOP1-1 were not shown here.

Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: darkness/light signaling; likely dark-induced nuclear accumulation as discussed
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: indirectly represses anthocyanin biosynthesis by destabilizing positive regulators
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: anthocyanin biosynthesis via proteostasis of transcriptional activators/coactivators
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: negative regulator that suppresses fruit coloration by ubiquitinating PavD8 and PavHY5
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inhibits anthocyanin accumulation by ubiquitinating and promoting degradation of PavD8 and PavHY5.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LCI; Y2H; pull-down; in vitro ubiquitination; cell-free degradation context
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PavCOP1-2 directly interacts with PavD8 and PavHY5. 2. It ubiquitinates PavD8 and PavHY5 in vitro. 3. Its action contributes to degradation of positive anthocyanin regulators/cofactors, especially in darkness.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light versus dark conditions; MG132 in degradation assay
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper infers light-induced nuclear depletion/cytosolic relocation consistent with COP1 function, but direct subcellular dynamics for PavCOP1-2 were not shown here.
